vulnerability.reference: Streaming services rely on a single NR-VQA model for bitrate decisions without redundancy.
vulnerability.generated: Monitoring systems trust learned quality metrics without adversarial integrity checks, leaving score manipulation undetected.

impact.reference: Degraded video quality drives viewer frustration, churn, and revenue loss.
impact.generated: Manipulated scores drive adaptive bitrate algorithms to degrade video streams, causing service-wide viewer quality of experience (QoE) degradation.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial perturbations enable arbitrary score changes that misguide bitrate algorithms in streaming, causing stuttering/blur, which drives viewer frustration, churn, and revenue loss.
dose_response.generated: Adversarial perturbations on NR-VQA models, through real-time quality monitoring by video streaming engineers, trigger unchecked trust in learned metrics, leading to manipulated scores that degrade adaptive bitrate algorithms and service-wide viewer QoE.
